zh.lifeSummary: 當Maria Hunt於1517年出生在英國瓦維其可爾斯圖德利時，她的父親Thomas Hunt27歲，母親Alice Hall27歲。她於1539年與Robert Fullwood在英國瓦維其可爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1588年2月17日在英國格盧斯特斯爾沙特波利享年71歲，埋葬在英國格盧斯特斯爾沙特波利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Maria Hunt于1517年出生在英国瓦維其可爾斯圖德利时，她的父亲Thomas Hunt27岁，母亲Alice Hall27岁。她于1539年与Robert Fullwood在英国瓦維其可爾结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1588年2月17日在英国格盧斯特斯爾沙特波利享年71岁，埋葬在英国格盧斯特斯爾沙特波利。

nameMeanings.name: Hunt
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (southwestern): occupational name for a hunter, from Middle English hunte ‘hunter, huntsman’ (Old English hunta). The term was used not only of the hunting on horseback of game such as stags and wild boars, which in the Middle Ages was a pursuit restricted to the ranks of the nobility, but also to much humbler forms of pursuit such as bird catching and poaching for food. The word seems also to have been used as an Old English personal name and to have survived into the Middle Ages as an occasional personal name. Compare Huntington and Huntley .
Irish: adopted for various Irish surnames containing or thought to contain the Gaelic element fiadhach ‘hunt’; for example Ó Fiaich (see Fee ) and Ó Fiachna (see Fenton ).
Possibly an Americanized form of German   Hundt  .

variants.name: Hunter
meanings: Scottish and northern English: occupational name from Middle English hunter(e) ‘huntsman’ (see Hunt ).
Irish (Antrim and Derry): adopted for Gaelic Ó Fiaich (see Fee ) due to confusion with the word fiadhach ‘hunt’.
History: A Scottish family of this name (see 1 above) has been established at Hunterston (Ayrshire) since the 13th century.

variants.name: Huntley
meanings: English: habitational name from Huntley in Gloucestershire, from Old English hunta ‘hunter, huntsman’ (see Hunt ) + lēah ‘woodland clearing’. Occasionally the name may arise from Huntley in Staffordshire, of the same etymology, but there is no medieval evidence that this gave rise to a surname.

Scottish: habitational name from a lost place called Huntlie in Berwickshire (Borders), with the same etymology as in 1 above. Huntly in Aberdeenshire was named for a medieval Earl of Huntly who took his title from the Borders placename, and is not the source of the surname.

English: occasionally perhaps a habitational name from Huntley in Preston on Wye (Herefordshire), first recorded in 1290 as a surname, (de) Huntelaw. The placename derives from Old English hunta ‘hunter, huntsman’ + hlāw ‘mound, hill’.

variants.name: Fenton
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places, in Yorkshire, Lincolnshire, Northumberland, Staffordshire, and Nottinghamshire, so called from Old English fenn ‘marsh, fen’ + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’.

Irish: English surname adopted by bearers of Gaelic Ó Fionnachta or Ó Fiachna ‘descendant of Fiachna’, an old personal name Anglicized as Feighney and sometimes mistranslated as Hunt (see Fee 1 and Finnerty ).
Scottish: habitational name from Fenton in East Lothian.

variants.name: Huntington
meanings: English (northern): habitational name from any of various places called Huntington, most of which are named with Old English huntena, the genitive plural form of hunta ‘hunter, huntsman’, + dūn ‘hill’ or tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Some, however, may have Old English hunting ‘hunting’ as the initial element, while others may derive from the Old English personal name Hunta (genitive Huntan), perhaps with Old English connective -ing-, + tūn. The surname may also arise from Huntingdon (Huntingdonshire), which derives from the Old English personal name Hunta (genitive Huntan) or Old English hunta (genitive huntan) + dūn.

History: A prominent American family of this name were founded by Simon Huntington, who himself never saw the New World, for he died in 1633 on the voyage to Boston, where his widow settled with her children. Their descendants include Jabez Huntington (1719–86), a wealthy West Indies trader, and Samuel Huntington (1731–96), who was one of the signers of the Declaration of Independence. Collis Potter Huntington (1821–1900) was an American railway magnate. Beginning with little education or money, he made a huge fortune, some of which he left to his nephew, Henry Huntington (1850–1927), who used the money to establish the Huntington library and art gallery in CA.

variants.name: Hunte
meanings: English: variant of   Hunt  . This form of the surname is common in the West Indies (mainly Barbados and Trinidad and Tobago) and Guyana.
North German: habitational name from the Hunte river in Lower Saxony.

nameMeanings.name: Maria
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Maria  , of German origin but now also moderately popular in the English-speaking world.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When Maria Hunt was born in 1517, in Studley, Warwickshire, England, her father, Thomas Hunt, was 27 and her mother, Alice Hall, was 27. She married Robert Fullwood in 1539, in Warwickshire, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. She died on 17 February 1588, in Saintbury, Gloucestershire, England, at the age of 71, and was buried in Saintbury, Gloucestershire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Maria Hunt est née en 1517, à Studley, Warwickshire, Angleterre, son père, Thomas Hunt, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Alice Hall, avait 27 ans. Elle a épousé Robert Fullwood en 1539, à Warwickshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 17 février 1588, à Saintbury, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 71 ans, et a été enterrée à Saintbury, Gloucestershire, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Maria Huntが1517年にイギリスワーウィクシュアイアストゥドリーで生まれた時，父Thomas Huntは27歳，母Alice Hallは27歳であった。1539年にイギリスワーウィクシュアイアでRobert Fullwoodと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1588年2月17日にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアサイントブリーで71歳で死去し，イギリスグロウセステルシュアイアサイントブリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Maria Hunt(이)가 1517년 영국 워윅실르 스투들리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Hunt(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice Hall(은)는 27세였다. 그녀는 1539년 영국 워윅실르에서 Robert Fullwood(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1588년 2월 17일 영국 그루스스터실르 세인트버이에서 71세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 그루스스터실르 세인트버이에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com Robert Fullwood em 1539, em Warwickshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 3 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Maria Hunt родилась в 1517 г. в местности студли, варвиккшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Thomas Hunt было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice Hall, 27. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert Fullwood в 1539 г. в местности варвиккшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла 17 февраля 1588 г. в местности сеинтбури, глоусестиршир, Англия в возрасте 71 год и была похоронена в местности сеинтбури, глоусестиршир, Англия.
